2603.17173.title: Generalist Multimodal LLMs Gain Biometric Expertise via Human Salience
2603.17173.paper_summary: Iris presentation attack detection (PAD) is critical for secure biometric deployments, yet developing specialized models faces significant practical barriers: collecting data representing future unknown attacks is impossible, and collecting diverse-enough data, yet still limited in terms of its predictive power, is expensive. Additionally, sharing biometric data raises privacy concerns. Due to rapid emergence of new attack vectors demanding adaptable solutions, we thus investigate in this paper whether general-purpose multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can perform iris PAD when augmented with human expert knowledge, operating under strict privacy constraints that prohibit sending biometric data to public cloud MLLM services. Through analysis of vision encoder embeddings applied to our dataset, we demonstrate that pre-trained vision transformers in MLLMs inherently cluster many iris attack types despite never being explicitly trained for this task. However, where clustering shows overlap between attack classes, we find that structured prompts incorporating human salience (verbal descriptions from subjects identifying attack indicators) enable these models to resolve ambiguities. Testing on an IRB-restricted dataset of 224 iris images spanning seven attack types, using only university-approved services (Gemini 2.5 Pro) or locally-hosted models (e.g., Llama 3.2-Vision), we show that Gemini with expert-informed prompts outperforms both a specialized convolutional neural networks (CNN)-based baseline and human examiners, while the locally-deployable Llama achieves near-human performance. Our results establish that MLLMs deployable within institutional privacy constraints offer a viable path for iris PAD.
2603.17173.paper_summary_zh: 虹膜呈現攻擊檢測（PAD）對於安全的生物識別部署至關重要，但開發專門模型面臨重大實際障礙：收集代表未來未知攻擊的數據是不可能的，而收集足夠多樣的數據又在其預測能力上受限，這是昂貴的。此外，共享生物識別數據會引發隱私問題。由於新攻擊向量的快速出現需要可適應的解決方案，因此我們在本文中探討一般用途的多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）在增強人類專家知識的情況下，是否能在嚴格的隱私約束下進行虹膜PAD，這些約束禁止將生物識別數據發送到公共雲MLLM服務。通過對應用於我們數據集的視覺編碼器嵌入進行分析，我們展示了在MLLMs中預訓練的視覺Transformer固有地將許多虹膜攻擊類型聚類，儘管從未明確訓練過這項任務。然而，在聚類顯示攻擊類別之間存在重疊的地方，我們發現結構化提示結合人類顯著性（來自識別攻擊指標的受試者的口頭描述）使這些模型能夠解決模糊性。在一個受到IRB限制的數據集中測試224張涵蓋七種攻擊類型的虹膜圖像，僅使用大學批准的服務（Gemini 2.5 Pro）或本地托管的模型（例如，Llama 3.2-Vision），我們顯示出使用專家知識提示的Gemini在性能上超越了基於專門卷積神經網絡（CNN）的基準和人類檢查員，而本地可部署的Llama則達到了接近人類的表現。我們的結果確立了在機構隱私約束內可部署的MLLMs為虹膜PAD提供了一條可行的途徑。
2603.17173.author: Jacob Piland et.al.
2603.17173.authors: Jacob Piland, Byron Dowling, Christopher Sweet, Adam Czajka
